PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Hosting guests
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: hosting as social labor and theater - write the colonist reading an outsider, working for trust, and minding the colony's reputation without inventing the guest's answer

instructions.li: hosting guests - write the effort behind warmth: attention, patience, charm, and the private read of a stranger inside the walls
hosting guests - keep the guest's response to what the rendered log actually supplies; the diary belongs to the colonist doing the work
hosting guests - let curiosity, caution, ambition, or exhaustion color the performance of welcome

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: welcoming but observant, hospitality treated as work

tones.li: socially alert and measured, warmth with a purpose
curious and guarded, an outsider close enough to study

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Guests scrounging food
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a hungry guest asking or scrounging for food - write the participating colonist's irritation, pity, embarrassment, or practical response without inventing motive

instructions.li: a guest scrounging - keep it small and human: food in hand, hunger visible, generosity or annoyance arriving before judgment
a guest scrounging - write only when a colonist actually shared the moment; never create a colony witness for guest-to-guest business

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: awkward and practical, hunger testing the welcome

tones.li: irritated but observant, hospitality wearing thin
quietly sympathetic, hunger making dignity difficult
